_extractionGuide.sampleLanguage: COLT 2018-4 Mortgage Pass-Through Certificates, Series 2018-4, Class A-1, Class A-2, Class A-3, Class M-1, Class M-2, Class B-1, Class B-2, Class B-3, Class X and Class R Certificates

extractionRules.classTag: SEN from 'Senior', MEZ from 'Mezzanine', SUB from 'Subordinate'
extractionRules.subordinationOrder: Assign incrementally based on table order, seniors first

_tranchesExtractionGuide.section: Certificate Table and Pass-Through Rate footnotes

sampleLanguage.balances: Class A-1 $243,005,000 ... The Initial Class Balance or Initial Class Notional Amount, as applicable, for each class of Offered Certificates is subject to adjustment up to plus or minus 5%
sampleLanguage.passThruRates: The Pass-Through Rate for the Class A-1, Class A-2, Class A-3, Class M-1, Class M-2 and Class B-1 Certificates for each Accrual Period will equal the lesser of (i) the fixed rate for such class set forth above and (ii) the Net WAC Rate for the related Payment Date.
sampleLanguage.variableRates: The Pass-Through Rate for the Class B-2 and Class B-3 Certificates for each Accrual Period will equal the Net WAC Rate for the related Payment Date.
sampleLanguage.classX: The Class X Certificates will not bear interest at a stated rate but will entitle the holder thereof to receive certain excess amounts

extractionRules.couponFormula: If 'lesser of (i) fixed rate and (ii) Net WAC Rate' -> use MIN(fixedRate, eff_wac). If only 'Net WAC Rate' -> use eff_wac
extractionRules.originalBalance: Extract dollar amounts from Certificate Table, remove $ and commas
extractionRules.legalMaturityDate: Use 'Final Scheduled Distribution Date', convert to YYYY-MM-DD format

triggers.description: Credit Enhancement Trigger Event (p.105) - CE falls below 50% of original

_triggersExtractionGuide.section: Definitions section, typically near 'Priority of Distributions'

sampleLanguage.creditEnhancement: A 'Credit Enhancement Trigger Event' has occurred on a Distribution Date if (I) the quotient of (a) the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans as of the last day of the prior Due Period less the Class Balance of the Class A-1 Certificates... over (b) the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans... is less than (II) the product of (a) 50% and (b) the quotient of (i) the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans as of the Cut-off Date (less the initial Class Balance of the Class A-1 Certificates on the Closing Date) over (ii) the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans as of the Cut-off Date.
sampleLanguage.cumulativeLoss: A 'Cumulative Loss Trigger Event' has occurred on a Distribution Date if the percentage equivalent of the aggregate Realized Losses from the Cut-off Date through the last day of the related Due Period divided by the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans as of the Cut-off Date exceeds the applicable percentages set forth below with respect to such Distribution Date: December 2018 through November 2019 2.00%, December 2019 through November 2020 3.00%, December 2020 through November 2022 5.50%, December 2022 and thereafter 10.00%
sampleLanguage.delinquency: A 'Delinquency Trigger Event' has occurred on a Distribution Date if the percentage equivalent of (a) the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans that are 60 days or more delinquent pursuant to the MBA delinquency methodology (including Mortgage Loans in foreclosure, REO property or bankruptcy status) and all Mortgage Loans subject to a servicing modification within the 12 months prior to such Distribution Date... as of the last day of the related Due Period, divided by (b) the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans as of the last day of the related Due Period exceeds the applicable percentages: December 2018 through November 2021 20%, December 2021 through November 2023 25%, December 2023 and thereafter 30%
sampleLanguage.optionalRedemption: The Depositor may, at its option, on any date on or after the earlier of (i) the two year anniversary of the Closing Date or (ii) the date on which the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans is reduced to 30% or less of the aggregate Stated Principal Balance of the Mortgage Loans as of the Cut-off Date (the 'Redemption Date'), purchase all of the outstanding Certificates

extractionRules.creditEnhancement: Look for 'Credit Enhancement Trigger Event' definition. Extract the percentage (e.g., 50%) and reference class (usually Class A-1). triggerType = CREDIT_ENHANCEMENT
extractionRules.cumulativeLoss: Look for 'Cumulative Loss Trigger Event' definition with schedule table. Extract date ranges and threshold percentages. Create scheduledVariables entries.
extractionRules.delinquency: Look for 'Delinquency Trigger Event' definition. Note the delinquency days (60, 90, etc.) and schedule. triggerType = DELINQ_TRIGGER_SUB_X where X = months (60 days = 2 months)
extractionRules.cleanUpCall: Look for optional redemption at pool balance percentage (e.g., '30% or less'). triggerType = COLLATERAL_VALUE, triggerParam = percentage

sampleLanguage.noTrigger: On each Distribution Date as to which none of a Credit Enhancement Trigger Event, a Cumulative Loss Trigger Event or a Delinquency Trigger Event is in effect, the Securities Administrator will allocate the Principal Remittance Amount in the following order of priority: first, sequentially, to the Class A-1, Class A-2 and Class A-3 Certificates, in that order, any unpaid Interest Distribution Amounts...; second, concurrently, to the Class A-1, Class A-2 and Class A-3 Certificates, pro rata, until their respective Class Balances are reduced to zero; third, to the Class M-1 Certificates...; fourth, to the Class M-1 Certificates, until the Class Balance thereof is reduced to zero; fifth, to the Class M-2...; sixth, to the Class M-2...; seventh, to the Class B-1...; eighth, to the Class B-1...; ninth, to the Class B-2...; tenth, to the Class B-2...; eleventh, to the Class B-3...; twelfth, to the Class B-3 Certificates until the Class Balance thereof is reduced to zero
sampleLanguage.withTrigger: On each Distribution Date as to which any of a Credit Enhancement Trigger Event, a Cumulative Loss Trigger Event or a Delinquency Trigger Event is in effect, the Securities Administrator will allocate the Principal Remittance Amount in the following order of priority: first, sequentially, to the Class A-1 and Class A-2 Certificates, in that order...; second, sequentially, to the Class A-1 and Class A-2 Certificates, in that order, until the Class Balances thereof have been reduced to zero; third, to the Class A-3...; fourth, to the Class A-3...; fifth, to the Class M-1...; sixth, to the Class M-1...; seventh, to the Class M-2...; eighth, to the Class M-2...; ninth, to the Class B-1...

extractionRules.proRata: Words like 'concurrently', 'pro rata' indicate PRORATA structure for those classes
extractionRules.sequential: Words like 'sequentially', 'in that order', 'first... second... third' indicate SEQ structure
extractionRules.triggerCondition: Look for 'as to which none of' (default/no trigger) vs 'as to which any of' (trigger fail). The triggers listed define which triggers affect the waterfall.
extractionRules.principalTypes: Usually scheduled, unscheduled (prepay), and recovery principal follow same structure. Look for 'same' or similar language.

sampleLanguage.cumulativeLossSchedule:
Distribution Date Occurring In | Percentage
December 2018 through November 2019 | 2.00%
December 2019 through November 2020 | 3.00%
December 2020 through November 2022 | 5.50%
December 2022 and thereafter | 10.00%
sampleLanguage.delinquencySchedule:
Distribution Date Occurring In | Percentage
December 2018 through November 2021 | 20%
December 2021 through November 2023 | 25%
December 2023 and thereafter | 30%

extractionRules.dateConversion: Convert 'December 2018' to '2018-12-01', 'November 2019' to '2019-11-30'. Use month start for beginDate, month end for endDate.
extractionRules.perpetualEndDate: For 'and thereafter', use far future date like '2100-11-30'

sampleLanguage.masterServicingFee: Master Servicing Fee... the greater of $3,500 and 0.029% per annum of the aggregate Stated Principal Balance

extractionRules.fixedFee: Fixed monthly amount -> use as formula directly
extractionRules.basisPoints: Convert basis points to decimal: 29 bps = 0.00029. Formula: begin_balance * rate / 12
